title: Cisco MCP Metadata Extension (x-cisco-metadata)
description: DevNet-standardized extension for MCP Server Description documents. Captures Cisco-specific runtime observations, dump provenance, CORS browser-readiness, pagination behavior, and connection diagnostics discovered by mcpcontract during capability extraction. Intended for use as the value of the 'x-cisco-metadata' extension property in an MCP Description document (mcpdesc 0.5.0+).

sourceFormat.description: Identifies the source format that produced this metadata

sourceSchemaVersion.description: Schema version of the source dump (e.g., 'https://developer.cisco.com/mcp_contract_dump/schema/0.3.6')

dumpDetails.description: Provenance metadata about the capability extraction that produced this description

toolName.description: Name of the tool used to create the dump (e.g., 'mcpcontract')

createdAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the capability extraction was performed

description.description: Optional human-readable description of how this dump was created or its purpose

serverConfig.description: Connection configuration used during capability extraction

name.description: Human-readable name of the server from config

env.description: Environment variable names used for stdio transport (values redacted)

runtimeObservations.description: Runtime observations discovered during MCP initialization and capability extraction

mcpProtocolUsed.description: MCP protocol version used by the dump tool when communicating with the server. Affects the structure and interpretation of the dump.

sessionIdSupported.description: Whether the server returned a session ID during initialization. Only applicable for streamable-http transport. True means the server is stateful and requires session tracking.

sessionIdHeader.description: Exact header name (with casing) used by the server for session management. Preserves exact server behavior for accurate client implementation. Common values: 'Mcp-Session-Id', 'MCP-Session-Id', 'mcp-session-id'.

pingSupported.description: Whether the server responded to a ping request. Ping is an optional MCP utility used for connection health monitoring and session keepalive.

pingLatencyMs.description: Round-trip latency in milliseconds for the ping request. Baseline connection performance without business logic overhead. Only present when pingSupported is true.

instructions.description: Server instructions from MCP InitializeResult. Describes how to use the server and its features, often used as a system prompt hint.

cors.description: CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) support detection for browser-based MCP clients. Only applicable for streamable-http and sse transports.

browserReady.description: Overall assessment: can this server be used from a browser? True if all CORS requirements are met, false if any requirement is missing, null if detection was skipped or failed.

responseHeaders.description: CORS headers from the initialize response

accessControlExposeHeaders.description: Headers exposed to the browser via Access-Control-Expose-Headers

preflight.description: Results from OPTIONS preflight request (if performed)

tested.description: Whether a preflight request was sent

status.description: HTTP status code from OPTIONS request (typically 200 or 204 for success)

accessControlAllowOrigin.description: Value of Access-Control-Allow-Origin header from preflight

paginationDetection.description: Tracks which capabilities use cursor-based pagination. Only includes capabilities where nextCursor was detected. Zero-overhead detection — no extra API calls needed.

clientCapabilities.description: Client capabilities that were sent by the dump tool during initialization. Documents what the client declared it supports, which may influence server behavior.

splitOperation.description: Split operation metadata — only present when this description was generated from a split dump output. Provides full audit trail of the split.

splitExecution.description: Counts and filter rules applied during the split

enrichment.description: Enrichment data from the dump — examples and additional content not part of the standard MCP Description

toolResponseExamples.description: Response examples per tool, keyed by tool name

resourceContentExamples.description: Content examples per resource, keyed by resource URI

promptArgumentExamples.description: Argument examples per prompt, keyed by prompt name. Each value is a map of argument name to example values.

paginationDetectionResult.description: Pagination detection result for a capability

paginationDetected.description: Whether nextCursor was seen when fetching this capability

pagesRetrieved.description: Number of pages fetched to get all items

totalItems.description: Total number of items after exhaustive fetch
